name: Fell Stinger
effects: If the user successfully knocks out a target with Fell Stinger, raise their Attack by 2 Combat Stages. Contest

name: First Impression
effects: You may only use First Impression with Priority upon joining an encounter; if you do, First Impression Flinches the target. Switching out resets the requirement of joining an encounter. Contest

name: Leech Life
effects: After the target takes damage, the user gains HP equal to half of the damage they dealt to the target. Contest

name: Lunge
effects: Lunge may be used as a Free Action at the end of a Sprint Maneuver taken as a Standard Action, as long as the user Shifted at least 3 meters in a straight line towards the target. When used this way, Lunge's target receives a -5 penalty to damage rolls for 1 round. Contest

name: Pollen Puff
effects: Once per Scene, Pollen Puff may instead be used on an ally; when used this way, the target recovers 50% of their maximum Hit Points and the Move has the Healing keyword. Contest

name: Brutal Swing
effects: Steady Performance

name: Dark Void
effects: The target falls Asleep. Once per Scene, Dark Void may be used as if its range were “Burst 5, Friendly” instead. Contest

name: Darkest Lariat
effects: This Move ignores the target’s positive Defense Combat Stages and all Damage Reduction. Contest

name: Hyperspace Fury
effects: Interrupts may not be triggered against Hyperspace Fury. The user’s Defense is lowered by -1 Combat Stage. Contest

name: Power Trip
effects: This Move gains +2 to its Damage Base for every positive Combat Stage held by the user, to a maximum of DB 20. Contest

name: Sucker Punch
effects: If an adjacent foe targets the user with a Damaging Attack, Sucker Punch may be used as an Interrupt Move against the triggering foe. Contest

name: Throat Chop
effects: The target cannot use Moves with the Sonic keyword for the next two turns. Contest

name: Clanging Scales
effects: The user adds half their Defense Stat to Clanging Scale's Damage Roll. After the Move is resolved, the user's Defense is lowered by 1 CS. Contest

name: Core Enforcer
effects: Each target has its Ability disabled until the end of the encounter. If a target has more than one Ability, you choose one of them to disable. Contest

name: Dragon Hammer
effects: Big Show Electric

name: Magnetic Flux
effects: All targets with the Plus or Minus Abilities receive +1 Defense and Special Defense Combat Stages. Contest

name: Parabolic Charge
effects: After the targets take damage, the user gains Hit Points equal to half of the total damage they dealt to all targets. Contest

name: Plasma Fists
effects: The target’s next damaging Normal Type Move instead becomes Electric Type. Contest

name: Thunder Wave
effects: Thunder Wave Paralyzes the target. Targets immune to Electric Attacks are immune to Thunder Wave’s effects. Contest

name: Zing Zap
effects: Zing Zap Flinches the target on 15+. Contest

name: Fleur Cannon
effects: Lower the user’s Special Attack 2 Combat Stages after damage. Contest

name: Floral Healing
effects: The target regains Hit Points equal to half of its full Hit Point value. If the field is Grassy Terrain, the target gains 2/3 of its full Hit Point value instead. Contest

name: Nature’s Madness
effects: The target loses 1/2 of their current Hit Points. Contest

name: Flying Press
effects: Flying Press may deal Flying Type damage if the user wishes. Contest

name: Burn Up
effects: Until the end of the encounter, the user loses its Fire-Type. Pure Fire-Types instead become Normal-Type. Contest

name: Fire Lash
effects: The target’s Defense is lowered by 1 Combat Stage. Contest

name: Mind Blown
effects: The user’s Hit Points are reduced by 50% of their full Hit Point value. This Hit Point loss cannot be prevented or reduced in any way. Contest

name: Shell Trap
effects: If the user is hit by a Melee attack, they may use Shell Trap as an Interrupt. Contest

name: Beak Blast
effects: Beak Blast must be declared at the start of the round. If the user is hit by a Melee attack this round, their attacker is Burned. At the end of the round, the user may shift and attack with Beak Blast. Contest

name: Moongeist Beam
effects: This Move ignores any Abilities that would cause it to fail or miss, such as Dodge or Wonder Guard; those Abilities cannot be activated in response to Moongeist Beam. Contest

name: Shadow Bone
effects: The target’s Defense is lowered by 1 Combat Stage on a 17+. Contest

name: Spectral Thief
effects: Before rolling damage, transfer all Combat Stages on the target to the user. Contest

name: Spirit Shackle
effects: The Target is Trapped for 2 rounds. Contest

name: Leafage
effects: Steady Performance

name: Solar Blade
effects: If the weather is not Sunny, the user gains +1 Combat Stage in Attack and Special Defense, and the user’s turn ends. If the weather is Sunny, immediately proceed to the Resolution Effect instead and this Move loses the Set-Up keyword.

name: Strength Sap
effects: The user gains Hit Points equal to the higher of the target's Attack or Special Attack; the target then loses 1 Combat Stage in that Stat. Contest

name: Trop Kick
effects: The target receives a -5 penalty to damage rolls for 1 round. Contest

name: High Horsepower
effects: High Horsepower may be used as a Free Action at the end of a Sprint Maneuver taken as a Standard Action, as long as the user Shifted at least 3 meters in a straight line towards the target. When used this way, High Horsepower gains Smite. Contest

name: Shore Up
effects: The user regains Hit Points equal to half of its full Hit Point value. If it is Sand Storming, the user gains 2/3 of its full Hit Point value instead. If it is Sunny, Rainy or Hailing the user gains 1/4 of their full Hit Point value instead. Contest

name: Stomping Tantrum
effects: If the user’s last attack failed or missed, Stomping Tantrum is DB 15 (4d10+10 / 45) instead. Contest

name: Thousand Arrows
effects: When calculating Weakness and Resistance for Thousand Arrows, Flying-Typed targets calculate damage as if Flying was neutral to Ground. Thousand Arrows ignores the Levitate ability. The target is knocked down to ground level, and loses all Sky or Levitate Speeds for 3 turns. During this time, they may be hit by Ground-Type Moves even if normally immune. Contest

name: Thousand Waves
effects: Legal Targets are Trapped for 2 rounds. Contest

name: Aurora Veil
effects: Blessing – Any user affected by Aurora Veil may activate it when receiving Damage to resist the Damage one step. Aurora Veil may be activated 2 times, and then disappears.

name: Ice Hammer
effects: The user’s Speed is lowered by 1 Combat Stage. Contest

name: Laser Focus
effects: The next successful damaging attack made by the user is automatically a Critical Hit. Contest

name: Multi-Attack
effects: Multi-Attack is the same Type as the Memory Disc, Plate, or Drive Item held. Contest

name: Revelation Dance
effects: Revelation Dance is the same Type as the user’s primary Type (the first one listed in the Pokédex). Revelation Dance deals +5 Bonus Damage for every other Dance Move used by the user this round, to a maximum of +15. Contest

name: Spotlight
effects: Until the end of the user’s next turn, the target is Blinded and Vulnerable. Contest

name: Swagger
effects: The target’s Attack is raised 2 Combat Stages. Swagger Confuses the target. Contest

name: Tackle
effects: The target is pushed 2 Meters. Contest

name: Tearful Look
effects: Tearful Look lowers all Legal Targets’ Attack and Special Attack 1 Combat Stage. Contest

name: Baneful Bunker
effects: If the user is hit by an attack, the user may use Baneful Bunker. The user is instead not hit by the Move. You do not take any damage nor are you affected by any of the Move’s effects. In addition, if the triggering attack was Melee ranged, the attacker is Poisoned. Contest

name: Purify
effects: The target is cured of all Permanent and Volatile Statuses. For every status removed, the user recovers a Tick of Hit Points. Contest

name: Toxic Thread
effects: The target is Poisoned and has their Speed lowered by 1 Combat Stage. If the target is already Poisoned, they instead lose a Tick of Hit Points and has their Speed lowered by 2 Combat Stages. Contest

name: Instruct
effects: The target immediately reuses the attack it performed last, ignoring frequency, as a Free Action. They may choose new targets for the copied attack. Instruct may not be used if the Target’s last Move was a Set-Up Move, a Trigger Move, or Instruct, or if the Target is currently affected by Exhaust. Contest

name: Hyperspace Hole
effects: Make and resolve of Hyperspace Hole’s attacks separately. In between each attack, the user may Shift using their Teleporter capability, ending the Shift next to a legal target. Interrupts may not be triggered against Hyperspace Hole. Contest

name: Photon Geyser
effects: This attack uses the highest of the user’s Attack or Special Attack Stat, though it remains a Special Move. This Move ignores any Abilities that would cause it to fail or miss, such as Dodge or Wonder Guard; those Abilities cannot be activated in response to Photon Geyser. Contest

name: Prismatic Laser
effects: Exhausting Act

name: Psychic Fangs
effects: Light Screen, Reflect and Aurora Veil cannot be declared in response to Psychic Fangs. Contest

name: Psychic Terrain
effects: The Field becomes Weird for five rounds. While the Field is Weird, non-Flying and non- Levitating Pokémon cannot declare Priority or Interrupt Moves outside their own Initiatives. Damaging Psychic-Type attacks deal an additional 10 damage Contest

name: Speed Swap
effects: The user and the target trade Initiative values. Contest

name: Accelerock
effects: Saving Grace Steel

name: Anchor Shot
effects: The Target is Trapped for 2 rounds. Anchor Shot may only be used from the user's Anchor Token. Contest

name: Gear Up
effects: All targets with the Plus or Minus Abilities receive +1 Attack and Special Attack Combat Stages Contest

name: Smart Strike
effects: Smart Strike cannot miss. Contest

name: Sunsteel Strike
effects: This Move ignores any Abilities that would cause it to fail or miss, such as Dodge or Wonder Guard; those Abilities cannot be activated in response to Sunsteel Strike. Contest

name: Liquidation
effects: The target’s Defense is lowered by 1 Combat Stage on a 17+. Contest

name: Sparkling Aria
effects: When you hit a target with Sparkling Aria, the user may choose to deal no damage and instead cure that target of Burn, Confusion, Infatuation, or Rage. Contest

name: Steam Eruption
effects: Steam Eruption burns all legal targets on a 15+. Contest

name: Water Shuriken
effects: Reliable
